title: Sorrow. A Quatrain.
content:
Death takes her hand and leads her through the waste
 Of her own soul, wherein she hears the voice
 Of lost Love's tears, and, famishing, can but taste
 The dead-sea fruit of Life's remembered joys.
meaning:
This evocative piece by Madison Julius Cawein, titled "Sorrow. A Quatrain.", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
